news.title: Real estate approvals need fewer gaps and faster coordination, says Karnataka RERA Chairman Rakesh Singh
news.para: Speaking at the ET Realty & Beyond Summit Bengaluru, Rakesh Singh said improving coordination between government agencies, regulators and developers will be critical to reducing project delays and improving housing delivery.

news.title: Bengaluru’s next growth phase will depend on planned expansion, not organic sprawl: Maheshwar Rao
news.para: Speaking at the ET Realty & Beyond Summit Bengaluru, Greater Bengaluru Authority Chief Commissioner Maheshwar Rao said the city’s long-term growth strategy will hinge on integrated planning, infrastructure investments, and new urban development corridors to accommodate a rapidly rising population.

news.title: Pune: Proposal to prepare draft development plan for merged areas gets general body's nod
news.para: Pune Municipal Corporation's general body has greenlit the preparation of a draft development plan for 23 areas merged in 2021. City engineer Aniruddha Pavaskar is appointed town planning officer for this crucial task.  This move aims to overcome past technical hurdles and ensure timely planning for these expanded territories, with surveys and public hearings forming key steps.

news.title: Goa: Karapur protest against housing project enters day 78
news.para: Villagers in Karapur are continuing their 78-day protest against a mega housing project, alleging environmental norm violations.  Under the Karapur Gramastha Ekvoticho Aawaz banner, residents, including resolute women, are staging a sit-in.  A tree-planting drive was initiated on Sunday in cleared areas, with hopes of wildlife returning to the restored ecosystem, as evidenced by a peacock sighting.

news.title: Ahmedabad civic body cannot revoke its own regularisation orders: Gujarat HC
news.para: The Gujarat High Court has ruled that once an authority grants or denies regularization of unauthorized construction by accepting an impact fee, it cannot later revoke that decision. The court quashed an Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation order that canceled a prior regularization, stating the authority lacked jurisdiction for such a review. This decision protects property owners from arbitrary cancellations after regularization.

news.title: Staff crunch leaves Ahmedabad civic body struggling to collect nominal property rents
news.para: Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation is facing a significant revenue loss as thousands of prime commercial properties are leased for as little as Rs 100-500 monthly.  A severe staff shortage has hampered rent collection, with many properties in disrepair.  To address this, the AMC is launching a digital overhaul and prioritizing redevelopment of underutilized prime real estate to boost commercial potential.

news.title: Goa: GIDC drafting new building bylaws to boost industrial space
news.para: Goa's Industrial Development Corporation is introducing new construction norms for industrial units to simplify permissions and maximize built-up space, addressing the state's dwindling industrial land.  Industries Minister Mauvin Godinho highlighted that while vertical expansion is now permitted with increased FAR, land scarcity in Goa necessitates efficient land utilization.  Industrialists are also advised to prepare for ongoing global economic challenges and environmental factors.

news.title: Andhra Pradesh plans rental housing projects for workers near industrial clusters
news.para: Andhra Pradesh is assessing demand for affordable rental housing for industrial workers, targeting 13 key clusters. This initiative, aligned with the Union government's scheme, aims to provide safe, quality accommodation for migrant workers and those in informal settlements. By addressing housing shortages, the state seeks to boost worker welfare, improve productivity, and attract further industrial investment.

news.title: Prepare groundwater policy: Bihar CM
news.para: Bihar Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary has directed officials to swiftly develop a groundwater policy for conservation and sustainable management of the state's water resources. Emphasizing the need for a robust institutional framework and coordinated departmental efforts, he urged acceleration of the proposed Bihar Groundwater Management and Regulation Act, 2026.

news.title: Can’t delay conveyance over future TDR use: Bombay HC
news.para: The Bombay High Court has ruled that housing societies can receive unilateral deemed conveyance of land, even if future development potential on public amenity plots is involved. Justice Sandeep Marne stated that conveyance shouldn't be delayed until the municipal corporation acquires such land. The court modified a previous order, directing conveyance of four acres instead of five, while allowing the builder to claim benefits like TDR for reserved land portions.

news.title: PMAY-Urban deadline extended till September-end in Odisha
news.para: The central government has extended the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Urban (PMAY-U) mission until September 30, 2026, marking it as the final deadline. Financial support will cease for projects not initiated by December 31, 2025. This directive urges states, including Odisha, to accelerate construction and ensure occupancy within the new timeframe, with no further extensions anticipated.

news.title: Mumbai: Homeowners of housing society in Mulund allege interior firms duped them
news.para: Homeowners in Mulund West are reeling from a suspected interior design scam, with multiple firms allegedly vanishing after collecting lakhs in advance payments.  Residents report projects abandoned midway or never started, leaving them with unfinished homes and significant financial losses.  Despite police advising civil action, victims are pursuing legal recourse, demanding a thorough investigation into what they fear is a coordinated fraud targeting eager homebuyers.

news.title: Can’t cancel registered sale deed unilaterally: Telangana HC tells GHMC
news.para: A woman's attempt to regularize a plot of land was rejected by the Telangana High Court, which agreed it was designated for public road use in a layout. However, the court also invalidated the subsequent unilateral cancellation of her registered sale deed, stating such actions require adherence to legal procedures. The court directed the land be added to a prohibitory register to prevent further dealings.

news.title: Drone surveys to redraw urban property maps across Maharashtra; 1.4 lakh mutation applications cleared online
news.para: Maharashtra is launching a massive drone survey to map urban properties and digitize land records, aiming to improve accuracy and reduce ownership disputes. This initiative will create high-resolution digital maps and streamline the mutation process for nearly one crore records. Citizens can now file property change requests online, track progress, and receive SMS alerts, making services faster and more transparent.

news.title: Kerala collected ₹2,178.28 crore as building construction cess in five years
news.para: Kerala's construction workers face a stark reality: while the government collected over Rs 2,178 crore in welfare cess over five years, nearly Rs 840 crore in pension arrears remain unpaid.  Despite a healthy closing balance, over 4 lakh registered workers haven't received full pension payments for a year. This raises serious questions about fund utilization, even as officials deny diversion.

news.title: MHADA’s 925-acre Mumbai redevelopment pipeline may draw ₹4 lakh crore investment
news.para: Mumbai's housing redevelopment is set for a massive Rs 4 lakh crore investment, promising 75,000 rehabilitation tenements and 30,000 MHADA units. This large-scale urban renewal, covering 925 acres across 11 projects, aims to transform ageing colonies and densely populated areas.  The new Construction & Development Agency model prioritizes residents with transit rent support and maintenance funds, attracting major developers like Adani and Reliance.

news.title: Bombay HC sets 60-day deadline for NCDRC to deliver verdict for DB Ozone residents
news.para: Bombay High Court has ordered the National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission to expedite hearings and deliver a judgment within 60 days for over 3,000 families in Dahisar East's DB Ozone. Residents have waited over 12 years for an occupancy certificate, hindering society formation. Developer Neelkamal Realtors cites issues with rental housing tenements, while residents allege ongoing maintenance fee collection without transparency.

news.title: GoI reconstitutes Chandigarh environmental clearance bodies; panels to vet large realty and civic projects
news.para: Chandigarh's environmental oversight has been revamped with a new three-year term for its Impact Assessment Authority and Expert Appraisal Committee. These bodies will now scrutinize urban development projects, from large residential complexes to commercial hubs and municipal infrastructure, ensuring strict adherence to environmental norms. The reconstituted panels, led by Dr. Harsh Mitter and Dr.

news.title: Building materials group CRH to buy Arcosa in $8.5 billion all-cash deal
news.para: Irish building materials giant CRH is set to acquire U.S.-based Arcosa for approximately $8.5 billion in an all-cash deal. This strategic move, offering a 10.4% premium, significantly expands CRH's North American footprint and taps into robust U.S. infrastructure demand, particularly in energy and utilities. Analysts suggest CRH might later spin off its European operations to focus purely on North America.
